source.name: Anu Varughese — 'Fresh Start — PROPERTY ROADMAP' (Google Sheets workbook, 'Property Roadmap')
source.captured: Screenshots of Google Meet product demo (1 Jun), received 16 Jul 2026 via WhatsApp
source.scope: PORTFOLIO-LEVEL external source: 16 property slots (4 filled), Summary tab Year 0-18 multi-property projection
unknowns: Cashflow/Commercial tab — never shown; contents unknown
'Other Expenses' breakdown on the Purchases sheet — section header visible, contents cut off; only the per-property annual totals ($2,940 / $2,979) are known
No explicit growth-rate cell visible anywhere — growth rates below are DERIVED by fitting her Portfolio Value curve, not transcribed
Columns beyond Year 18 on the Summary (if any)
P5 column was demo-only (added then absent in later frames) — excluded from this fixture; the Year 0-18 Summary table corresponds to the 4-property portfolio

portfolioSummaryYear0to18.note: Transcribed verbatim from the Summary tab (Section 5 of the transcription). Interest/Operating/Cashflow rows were displayed as negatives in red parentheses; stored signed here. Year index 0-18.

derivedNotTranscribed.note: No growth-rate, escalation, or compounding cell is visible in any screenshot. Everything in this block was INFERRED by fitting her displayed numbers; the fit reproduces all 6 Summary rows x 19 years to within $1 (cashflow within $0.89). Verified in run-anu-comparison.ts (Anu-replica section).

capitalGrowth.ratePctByOwnershipYear: 10% in ownership years 1 and 2, 7% in ownership year 3 and every year after
capitalGrowth.fitMethod: Year-over-year Portfolio Value ratios between purchases: Y6->Y7 onward = 1.0700 exactly (all properties past year 2). Per-property back-out: Year 3 value = P1*1.1*1.1*g3 + P2*1.1*1.1 + P3*1.1 + P4 solves g3=1.07; forward-check reproduces Y4 ($2,860,016) and Y5 ($3,080,017) to the dollar with 10/10/7/7...
capitalGrowth.appliedFromFirstOwnershipYear: Value shown at cost in the first owned Summary year; growth applied from the following year

operatingCostEscalation.managementFee: 7% of (escalated) gross rent — rides the 5% rent escalation
operatingCostEscalation.flatBlockFit: insurance+rates+other ($5,690 for P1) inflates at 3.0%/yr by ownership year: Y1 opex 15,282 = 7,510 + (1,820*1.05 + 5,690*1.03); Y2 23,546 and all later years reproduce at 3.0% (exact)

interestConvention.ioForever: Interest flat at $113,770 from Year 3 through Year 18 — loans never amortise; Equity = Portfolio Value minus constant $1,840,000 debt

derivedNotTranscribed.vacancy: No vacancy row anywhere; gross rent flows straight into cashflow
derivedNotTranscribed.noTaxModelling: No negative-gearing / after-tax line, no land tax, no CGT, no equity recycling visible on the Summary
derivedNotTranscribed.otherExpensesLine: UNKNOWN source. $2,940 (P1/P2, $550k) vs $2,979 (P3/P4, $600k) — not proportional to value or rent; the 'Other Expenses' breakdown section was never shown. Values used verbatim as a flat annual opex line.

engineMappingForComparison.note: How run-anu-comparison.ts maps the above into PropPath engine inputs — every field set explicitly.
engineMappingForComparison.purchaseYearMapping: Her Summary Year 0 -> calendar 2026 (P1 manually placed at period 3). P2 2027, P3 2028, P4 2029. 2026+ start chosen deliberately: all four purchases are then post-NG-reform established buys, so the engine's negative-gearing benefit is $0 and its cashflow line is pre-tax like hers.
engineMappingForComparison.growthCurveMapping: Engine tier structure is year1 / years2to3 / year4 / year5plus (years 2 AND 3 share a rate) and cannot express her 10/10/7/7...; best-fit custom curve via assumptions: year1=10, years2to3=sqrt(1.10*1.07)-1=8.48963% (geometric mean so ownership-year-3 cumulative value is exact), year4=7, year5plus=7. Exact at every integer ownership year except year 2 (transient -1.37% dip per property). Nearest BUILT-IN tier is High (12.5/10/7.5/6) — compared separately.

interestVariants.A-as-stated: instance interestRate 6.00 (our simple annual-interest convention: loan x 6.00%)
interestVariants.B-her-convention: instance interestRate 6.18313 (her daily-compounded effective rate) to isolate the convention gap

engineMappingForComparison.opexMapping: propertyManagementPercent 7, buildingInsuranceAnnual 1250, councilRatesWater 1500, strata 0, maintenanceAllowanceAnnual = her 'Other Expenses' (2940/2979 — the only flat opex slot left; her breakdown unknown), landTaxOverride 0
engineMappingForComparison.purchaseCostMapping: engagementFee 19500, conveyancing 2500, all other one-off cost fields 0, stampDutyOverride = her transcribed figure (our calculator's own figures compared separately per state)
engineMappingForComparison.loanMapping: lvr 80, loanProduct IO, ioTermYears 30, loanTerm 30, lmiWaiver false (LMI $0 at 80% LVR), lmiCapitalized false. Note: the engine's PORTFOLIO-level debt/interest lines hold planned loans at the original loan amount regardless of ioTermYears (IO-forever at portfolio level), which matches her sheet by construction.
engineMappingForComparison.profileDials: vacancyRate 0 (profile AND per-instance) to match her missing vacancy row — noted as HER modelling gap, not ours; rentEscalationRate 0.05; inflationRate 0.03; interestRate matches variant; funding made generous (depositPool 900k, savings 60k/yr, salary 350k, BC $5m) so placement gating cannot contaminate the projection comparison
